diagnosis: The dual_ma strategy implements a classic fast/slow moving-average crossover with three filters: ADX trend-strength gate (adx_14 >= 26.5), volume confirmation (volume_ratio >= 1.07), and price-above-slow-MA confirmation. A golden cross (fast MA crosses above slow MA) emits a LONG signal; a death cross emits a SHORT signal. The evolved params use EMA(20)/EMA(46), regime gate restricted to ["trend","breakout"], wide ATR stops (3.48x) and wide take-profits (5.64x), with a 36-hour time stop. The strategy DOES emit short signals (lines 89-99 in dual_ma.py). The short branch must be dropped for Phase-3 long-only compliance. The core edge is trend-following: capture sustained directional moves in crypto bull markets. The weakness is the classic MA crossover lag — entries occur after a move has already begun, producing whipsaw losses during choppy/ranging markets. The evolved regime filter ("trend","breakout" only) partially mitigates ranging losses but also reduces trade count. With only 41 trades in the evolved in-sample run, the statistical base is thin and the strategy is at meaningful overfitting risk on the exact filter parameters.
long_only_hypothesis: Crypto markets exhibit persistent, multi-week uptrends driven by adoption cycles, macro risk-on flows, and halving-related supply shocks. A golden-cross signal on EMA(fast)/EMA(slow) — conditioned on confirmed trend strength (ADX > threshold), elevated volume (confirming breakout participation), and price already above the slow MA — identifies the moment when momentum is both sustained and broadly participated. In long-only spot, the edge is: enter when trend is confirmed and step to cash (not short) when the trend inverts. The regime filter restricts entries to periods where trend-following historically outperforms buy-and-hold on a risk-adjusted basis, reducing drawdown during BTC bear/ranging phases without requiring leverage or shorting. This long-or-cash implementation captures the upside convexity of crypto while protecting capital during confirmed downtrends by simply sitting in USDC.
market_techniques: ADX trend-strength gating to filter choppy regimes
Volume-ratio confirmation to distinguish breakout from noise
Regime classification gate (trend/breakout) to restrict entries to high-probability environments
ATR-scaled stops and take-profits for volatility-adaptive risk management
Time stop to prevent indefinite capital lock-up in stalled trades
Bull Market Support Band (BMSB) as macro regime overlay to suppress entries during structural bear markets
RSI momentum filter to avoid overbought entries
OBV divergence as secondary volume trend confirmation
Volatility squeeze detection (BB bandwidth percentile) to avoid entering during pre-squeeze compression with ambiguous direction
useful_features: trend: adx_14 — primary trend-strength gate, already in use
trend: bmsb_bullish — macro regime overlay; restrict longs to bars where price > both BMSB SMA and EMA
trend: roc_12 — rate of change over 12 bars as momentum confirmation
momentum: rsi_14 — avoid entering above RSI 70 (overbought filter) to improve entry timing
momentum: price_acceleration — confirm that the crossover is backed by accelerating price movement
volume: volume_ratio — already in use; ratio of current volume to 20-bar SMA
volume: obv_divergence — secondary confirmation that OBV trend agrees with price trend
volume: volume_zscore — detect genuine volume spikes vs. noisy volume_ratio
volatility: atr_14 — already in use for stop/TP scaling
volatility: bb_bw_percentile — rolling percentile of BB bandwidth; avoid entries during volatility compression (percentile < 20) where direction is undetermined
volatility: gk_vol — Garman-Klass volatility for vol-targeting position sizing
mean-reversion: bb_pct_b — avoid entries at extreme bb_pct_b > 0.9 (already stretched)
market-regime: regime parameter passed by engine — existing trend/breakout filter
drawdown: dist_from_high_20 — as a secondary confirmation; entries near 20-bar highs are more valid breakouts
microstructure: pressure_imbalance — buying_pressure - selling_pressure; positive imbalance corroborates bullish crossover
market-regime: ret_zscore_20 — zscore of recent returns; filter out entries at extreme zscore (already overbought spike)

genome_params.name: fast_period
genome_params.note: Fast EMA/SMA period; extending upper bound slightly from current registry [5,25] to allow for slower fast MA

genome_params.name: slow_period
genome_params.note: Slow EMA/SMA period; extended upper bound to explore wider separations; must enforce fast_period < slow_period in evaluation

genome_params.name: signal_type
genome_params.note: MA type: 'sma' or 'ema'; current evolved value is ema; sweep both

genome_params.name: adx_min
genome_params.note: Minimum ADX for trend confirmation; current evolved value 26.5; range allows exploring sensitivity

genome_params.name: volume_threshold
genome_params.note: Minimum volume_ratio to confirm breakout participation

genome_params.name: stop_loss_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for stop; evolved value 3.48 is wide; explore tighter stops for long-only where asymmetry differs

genome_params.name: take_profit_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for take-profit; evolved value 5.64; wide TP allows trends to run

genome_params.name: time_stop_hours
genome_params.note: Maximum hours to hold a stalled position; current 36h may be too short for multi-day trend moves

genome_params.name: rsi_max_entry
genome_params.note: NEW: upper RSI threshold to reject overbought entries; prevents chasing peaks

genome_params.name: bmsb_filter
genome_params.note: NEW: require bmsb_bullish == True (price above both 20-SMA and 21-EMA macro bands) as macro regime gate

genome_params.name: bb_bw_percentile_min
genome_params.note: NEW: minimum BB bandwidth percentile to avoid entering during volatility squeeze compression

genome_params.name: pullback_tolerance_pct
genome_params.note: Pct tolerance for price-above-slow-MA check; small values tighten the filter

do_not_optimize: require_trend_regime — the boolean itself should stay True (always filter by regime); only the allowed_regimes list content matters and it should be fixed to ['trend','breakout'] not swept
allowed_regimes — the list of allowed regimes is a categorical with only 4 possible values across 2^4 subsets; sweeping it massively overfits to historical regime labels
require_adx — fixing to True removes one binary degree of freedom; ADX filtering is structurally sound and removing it risks adding noise trades
require_volume — fixing to True likewise; volume confirmation is a well-grounded structural filter
require_price_above_slow — fixing to True is a sound structural constraint (price must be above trend anchor); sweeping it invites mean-reversion entries disguised as trend entries
time_stop_hours at granular resolution — fix to a round number (48h or 72h) rather than letting the genome pick e.g. 37h, which is a curve-fitted artifact on specific historical bars

risk_management.stop: ATR-scaled hard stop at entry_price - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr_14; current evolved multiplier 3.48 is reasonable for crypto volatility; floor at 1.5x ATR minimum to prevent hairline stops being gapped through
risk_management.take_profit: ATR-scaled take-profit at entry_price + take_profit_atr_mult * atr_14; evolved 5.64x lets trends run; for long-only spot where upside is unlimited, consider partial-take (50% at 3x ATR, trail remainder) rather than fixed TP
risk_management.trailing: Optional trailing stop: after price moves 2x ATR in favor, trail stop at entry_price + 1x ATR (breakeven + cushion); this captures extended trend moves that would otherwise be clipped by fixed TP at 5.64x
risk_management.vol_target: Volatility-targeted sizing via gk_vol or realized_vol_20: target 20% annualized portfolio volatility; position_size = equity * (vol_target / asset_annualized_vol) capped at max_position_pct; this dynamically reduces size during high-vol regimes (avoids oversizing into volatile breakouts)
risk_management.max_size: Max position size = 80% of equity (spot long-only; no leverage; keeping 20% as USDC buffer for costs and drawdown); risk_config.max_position_pct = 0.80
risk_management.cooldown: Minimum 12-hour cooldown after any trade exit before next entry; prevents rapid re-entry after stop-out into the same choppy regime; consider 24h cooldown after stop_loss exit specifically
risk_management.risk_off: BMSB macro gate: if bmsb_bullish == False (price below 20-SMA or 21-EMA long-period bands) suspend all new long entries and go to USDC; additionally gate on regime != ['ranging','bear'] from the engine regime classifier — these two gates together form a structural bear-market shield

metrics: CAGR — primary return metric; target >20% annualized in OOS 2024-2026 window
Sharpe ratio (annualized, hourly returns) — target >1.2 after costs; evolved IS Sharpe was 1.84, WF Sharpe 3.13 (unusually high, warrants scrutiny for data-snooping)
Sortino ratio — more relevant for long-only (only downside vol penalized); target >1.5
Maximum drawdown % — peak-to-trough on full equity curve; target <25% for spot long-only
Hit rate (% winning trades) — with wide stops (3.48 ATR) and wide TPs (5.64 ATR) expect 40-50% hit rate; below 35% signals stop-hunting
Average trade PnL net of all costs (commission + slippage + funding=0 for spot) — must be positive after realistic 5bps commission + 5bps slippage per side
Trade count — with 41 trades in the evolved run on multi-year data, statistical confidence is very low; target minimum 80 OOS trades for meaningful inference
Turnover / exposure time — fraction of time in position; spot long-only with time stops should sit in cash during downtrends; track as signal of regime-filtering effectiveness
Cost sensitivity — sweep commission from 2bps to 15bps and slippage from 2bps to 20bps; if Sharpe degrades below 0.8 at realistic costs, strategy is marginal
Walk-forward Sharpe stability — Sharpe across individual WF folds should not have std > 1.5 * mean; high variance means the strategy only works in specific sub-periods
Bootstrap / Monte Carlo — resample trade returns with replacement (1000 iterations) to get 5th-percentile Sharpe and CAGR; report confidence interval
Per-year Sharpe and CAGR — break out 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023 performance; 2022 bear year is the critical stress test for long-only spot
OOS degradation % — compute_oos_degradation(train_metrics, test_metrics) from engine; target <50% degradation IS to OOS
sweep_changes: DROP the short branch entirely (lines 89-99 in dual_ma.py): remove the elif block for death-cross short signal; death cross = exit to cash only (position becomes None, no new signal emitted — the engine's time stop and regime filter handle the exit naturally)

Add long-only exit signal: when a death cross occurs while long, emit Signal(side='long_exit') or return a signal with side opposite to position to trigger signal_exit in backtester — verify this triggers the 'signal_exit' path in the engine (line 302 of backtester.py: signal.side != position.side)
Add BMSB macro filter: at bar_idx check current.get('bmsb_bullish', True); if False and bmsb_filter param is True, return None (no new longs)
Add RSI overbought filter: if rsi_14 > rsi_max_entry param (default 70.0), return None to avoid chasing peaks
Add BB bandwidth percentile filter: if bb_bw_percentile < bb_bw_percentile_min param (default 20.0), return None to avoid entries during volatility compression with unclear direction
Fix require_adx = True, require_volume = True, require_price_above_slow = True as structural constants (remove from genome, hard-code in PARAMS)
Fix allowed_regimes = ['trend', 'breakout'] as structural constant (remove from genome)
Extend slow_period genome upper bound from 60 to 80 to allow exploration of slower trend-following periods suitable for multi-week crypto trends
Add time_stop_hours to genome with range [24, 120] at integer granularity (currently fixed at 36h; longer time stops may capture multi-day moves better)
Enforce fast_period < slow_period constraint in genome evaluation: if sampled fast >= slow, skip evaluation and assign worst-case fitness

Switch position sizing to volatility-targeted using gk_vol feature: position_size = min(equity * vol_target / asset_vol, equity * max_position_pct); add vol_target as a fixed param (not swept) at 0.20 annualized
Add 24h cooldown tracking: record last exit timestamp in strategy state or check bar timestamps; block new entries within 12-24h of last exit

Update STRATEGY_REGISTRY param_space in auto_evolve.py to: add rsi_max_entry (float, 60.0, 80.0), bmsb_filter (bool), bb_bw_percentile_min (float, 10.0, 40.0), time_stop_hours (int, 24, 120), pullback_tolerance_pct (float, 0.1, 1.0); remove require_adx, require_volume, require_price_above_slow, require_trend_regime from genome (fix them True)
methodological_risks: Thin trade count (41 trades in evolved IS run): with ~41 trades any Sharpe estimate has a standard error of ~1/sqrt(41) ≈ 0.16 — the reported IS Sharpe 1.84 has a 95% CI of roughly [1.5, 2.2]; OOS Sharpe 3.13 on the same or fewer trades is statistically implausible and strongly suggests data-snooping or regime coincidence

Walk-forward Sharpe 3.13 > IS Sharpe 1.84 is a red flag: genuine OOS performance should degrade, not improve; this can occur when the test window happened to coincide with a strong bull run (e.g. late 2023 rally) that the strategy was accidentally tuned to, rather than true generalization

EMA window computed on a slice df['close'].iloc[bar_idx - slow_period - 1 : bar_idx + 1] inside the loop (lines 56-59 of dual_ma.py): this recalculates EMA from scratch on a sub-window each bar, which means the EMA does NOT have the same history as if computed from bar 0; the warm-up effect makes the EMA values at crossover slightly different from a full-history EMA — this is a subtle but real calculation artifact that can affect signal timing

Regime label leakage risk: the regime parameter is passed externally by the engine; if the regime classifier uses future bars or is calibrated on the full dataset including OOS, entries conditioned on regime='trend' inherit that leakage

Parameter count vs. trade count: with 12 genome parameters and 41 trades, the model has ~1 free parameter per 3-4 trades — extreme overfitting territory; fixing 5 structural params reduces this but the problem remains
ADX threshold at exactly 26.5476 (an evolved decimal) is a classic overfitting signature; a round number (25 or 30) would be more robust; the decimal precision should be noted and rounded
Time stop at exactly 36 hours is similarly suspiciously precise; if the best training trades happened to resolve within 36h, this parameter is curve-fitted to historical bar sequences
Volume_ratio threshold 1.0685 at 4 decimal places is another overfitting artifact from the genetic algorithm; should be rounded to 1.1 for robustness testing

Cost model validation: the backtester charges commission + slippage but spot trades have no funding cost; verify funding_bps_per_8h is set to 0.0 for spot-only simulation (the Backtester defaults to 1.0 bps/8h which is a perp cost and inflates apparent net returns when excluded)

2022 bear market survivorship: a strategy tuned on 2019-2023 data that uses a regime filter for 'trend' only may have survived 2022 by sitting mostly flat; this reduces drawdown but also means the OOS 2024-2026 window (which includes another bull run) may look artificially good simply because crypto trended up again — not because the strategy has genuine alpha
